verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi det te fratrem meum,  sugentem ubera matris meæ,  ut inveniam te foris, et deosculer te,  et jam me nemo despiciat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֤י יִתֶּנְךָ֙ כְּאָ֣ח לִ֔י יוֹנֵ֖ק שְׁדֵ֣י אִמִּ֑י אֶֽמְצָאֲךָ֤ בַחוּץ֙ אֶשָּׁ֣קְךָ֔ גַּ֖ם לֹא־יָבֻ֥זוּ לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: O that thou wert as my brother, that sucked the breasts of my mother! when I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; yea, I should not be despised.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apprehendam te, et ducam in domum matris meæ :  ibi me docebis,  et dabo tibi poculum ex vino condito,  et mustum malorum granatorum meorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶנְהָֽגְךָ֗ אֲבִֽיאֲךָ֛ אֶל־בֵּ֥ית אִמִּ֖י תְּלַמְּדֵ֑נִי אַשְׁקְךָ֙ מִיַּ֣יִן הָרֶ֔קַח מֵעֲסִ֖יס רִמֹּנִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I would lead thee, and bring thee into my mother’s house, who would instruct me: I would cause thee to drink of spiced wine of the juice of my pomegranate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Læva ejus sub capite meo,  et dextera illius amplexabitur me.
verses.king_james: His left hand should be under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjuro vos, filiæ Jerusalem,  ne suscitetis, neque evigilare faciatis dilectam,  donec ipsa velit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִשְׁבַּ֥עְתִּי אֶתְכֶ֖ם בְּנ֣וֹת יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם מַה־תָּעִ֧ירוּ ׀ וּֽמַה־תְּעֹ֥רְר֛וּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָ֖ה עַ֥ד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּֽץ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, until he please.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ est ista quæ ascendit de deserto, deliciis affluens,  innixa super dilectum suum ?     Sub arbore malo suscitavi te ;  ibi corrupta est mater tua,  ibi violata est genitrix tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֣י זֹ֗את עֹלָה֙ מִן־הַמִּדְבָּ֔ר מִתְרַפֶּ֖קֶת עַל־דּוֹדָ֑הּ תַּ֤חַת הַתַּפּ֙וּחַ֙ עֽוֹרַרְתִּ֔יךָ שָׁ֚מָּה חִבְּלַ֣תְךָ אִמֶּ֔ךָ שָׁ֖מָּה חִבְּלָ֥ה יְלָדַֽתְךָ׃
verses.king_james: Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved? I raised thee up under the apple tree: there thy mother brought thee forth: there she brought thee forth that bare thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pone me ut signaculum super cor tuum,  ut signaculum super brachium tuum,  quia fortis est ut mors dilectio,  dura sicut infernus æmulatio :  lampades ejus lampades ignis atque flammarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׂימֵ֨נִי כַֽחוֹתָ֜ם עַל־לִבֶּ֗ךָ כַּֽחוֹתָם֙ עַל־זְרוֹעֶ֔ךָ כִּֽי־עַזָּ֤ה כַמָּ֙וֶת֙ אַהֲבָ֔ה קָשָׁ֥ה כִשְׁא֖וֹל קִנְאָ֑ה רְשָׁפֶ֕יהָ רִשְׁפֵּ֕י אֵ֖שׁ שַׁלְהֶ֥בֶתְיָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death; jealousy is cruel as the grave: the coals thereof are coals of fire, which hath a most vehement flame.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aquæ multæ non potuerunt extinguere caritatem,  nec flumina obruent illam.  Si dederit homo omnem substantiam domus suæ pro dilectione,  quasi nihil despiciet eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֣יִם רַבִּ֗ים לֹ֤א יֽוּכְלוּ֙ לְכַבּ֣וֹת אֶת־הָֽאַהֲבָ֔ה וּנְהָר֖וֹת לֹ֣א יִשְׁטְפ֑וּהָ אִם־יִתֵּ֨ן אִ֜ישׁ אֶת־כׇּל־ה֤וֹן בֵּיתוֹ֙ בָּאַהֲבָ֔ה בּ֖וֹז יָב֥וּזוּ לֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Many waters cannot quench love, neither can the floods drown it: if a man would give all the substance of his house for love, it would utterly be contemned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Soror nostra parva,  et ubera non habet ;  quid faciemus sorori nostræ  in die quando alloquenda est ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָח֥וֹת לָ֙נוּ֙ קְטַנָּ֔ה וְשָׁדַ֖יִם אֵ֣ין לָ֑הּ מַֽה־נַּעֲשֶׂה֙ לַאֲחֹתֵ֔נוּ בַּיּ֖וֹם שֶׁיְּדֻבַּר־בָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: We have a little sister, and she hath no breasts: what shall we do for our sister in the day when she shall be spoken for?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si murus est,  ædificemus super eum propugnacula argentea ;  si ostium est, compingamus illud tabulis cedrinis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־חוֹמָ֣ה הִ֔יא נִבְנֶ֥ה עָלֶ֖יהָ טִ֣ירַת כָּ֑סֶף וְאִם־דֶּ֣לֶת הִ֔יא נָצ֥וּר עָלֶ֖יהָ ל֥וּחַ אָֽרֶז׃
verses.king_james: If she be a wall, we will build upon her a palace of silver: and if she be a door, we will inclose her with boards of cedar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego murus, et ubera mea sicut turris,  ex quo facta sum coram eo, quasi pacem reperiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֣י חוֹמָ֔ה וְשָׁדַ֖י כַּמִּגְדָּל֑וֹת אָ֛ז הָיִ֥יתִי בְעֵינָ֖יו כְּמוֹצְאֵ֥ת שָׁלֽוֹם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: I am a wall, and my breasts like towers: then was I in his eyes as one that found favour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vinea fuit pacifico in ea quæ habet populos :  tradidit eam custodibus ;  vir affert pro fructu ejus mille argenteos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֶּ֣רֶם הָיָ֤ה לִשְׁלֹמֹה֙ בְּבַ֣עַל הָמ֔וֹן נָתַ֥ן אֶת־הַכֶּ֖רֶם לַנֹּטְרִ֑ים אִ֛ישׁ יָבִ֥א בְּפִרְי֖וֹ אֶ֥לֶף כָּֽסֶף׃
verses.king_james: Solomon had a vineyard at Baal–hamon; he let out the vineyard unto keepers; every one for the fruit thereof was to bring a thousand pieces of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vinea mea coram me est.  Mille tui pacifici,  et ducenti his qui custodiunt fructus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּרְמִ֥י שֶׁלִּ֖י לְפָנָ֑י הָאֶ֤לֶף לְךָ֙ שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה וּמָאתַ֖יִם לְנֹטְרִ֥ים אֶת־פִּרְיֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: My vineyard, which is mine, is before me: thou, O Solomon, must have a thousand, and those that keep the fruit thereof two hundred.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ habitas in hortis, amici auscultant ;  fac me audire vocem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַיּוֹשֶׁ֣בֶת בַּגַּנִּ֗ים חֲבֵרִ֛ים מַקְשִׁיבִ֥ים לְקוֹלֵ֖ךְ הַשְׁמִיעִֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken to thy voice: cause me to hear it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuge, dilecte mi, et assimilare capreæ,  hinnuloque cervorum super montes aromatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרַ֣ח ׀ דּוֹדִ֗י וּֽדְמֵה־לְךָ֤ לִצְבִי֙ א֚וֹ לְעֹ֣פֶר הָֽאַיָּלִ֔ים עַ֖ל הָרֵ֥י בְשָׂמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a roe or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices.
